gloss.en: very loud, like thunder

words.example: The crowd gave a thunderous round of applause.

gloss.en: a small mark showing something is correct

words.example: Put a tick next to each correct answer.

gloss.en: fixed firmly; hard to move or loosen

words.example: He kept a tight grip on her arm.

gloss.en: in a firm or close way

words.example: She held the rope tightly with both hands.

gloss.en: a flat square for covering floors or walls

words.example: They put a new tile on the bathroom wall.

gloss.en: up to a certain time; until

words.example: We're open till 6 o'clock.

gloss.en: a metal container for selling food

words.example: Open the tin and drain the tuna.

gloss.en: very small in size or amount

words.example: She held a tiny baby in her arms.

gloss.en: a small piece of practical advice

words.example: Here is a handy tip for buying a computer.

gloss.en: to become or make tired

words.example: Long walks tire the children quickly.

gloss.en: making you feel tired

words.example: It was a long and tiring journey.

gloss.en: one of the five parts at the foot's end

words.example: I hurt my big toe on the door.

gloss.en: the soft moving part inside the mouth

words.example: He clicked his tongue to attract their attention.

gloss.en: a substance used to clean your teeth

words.example: I put some toothpaste on my brush.

gloss.en: a violent storm with very strong spinning wind

words.example: A tornado destroyed several houses in the town.

gloss.en: completely; in every way

words.example: They come from totally different cultures.

gloss.en: the sense that feels things through the skin

words.example: We learn about the world through the sense of touch.

gloss.en: a journey visiting several places for pleasure

words.example: We went on a sightseeing tour of the city.

gloss.en: the business of serving people who travel for pleasure

words.example: The area is heavily dependent on tourism.

gloss.en: loose clothes worn for sport or exercise

words.example: He wore a tracksuit to the gym.

gloss.en: the buying and selling of goods or services

words.example: The two countries have a lot of foreign trade.

gloss.en: in a way that follows old customs

words.example: This dish is traditionally served at New Year.

gloss.en: relating to a change from one state to another

words.example: The country is in a transitional period of reform.

gloss.en: to put meaning into another language

words.example: He translated the letter into English.

gloss.en: a system for carrying people or goods

words.example: Public transport in the city is cheap and fast.

gloss.en: things you throw away; rubbish

words.example: Please take the trash out tonight.

gloss.en: to behave a certain way toward someone

words.example: You should treat people with respect.

gloss.en: the care given to cure an illness or injury

words.example: The drug is used in the treatment of depression.

gloss.en: to shake slightly, often from fear or cold

words.example: Her hands began to tremble with fear.

gloss.en: a general direction in which things change

words.example: There is a growing trend toward online shopping.

gloss.en: an act to deceive or fool someone

words.example: They had to think of a trick to get past the guards.

gloss.en: a small cut to make something neat

words.example: My hair needs a quick trim.

gloss.en: a brass musical instrument you blow into

words.example: He learned to play the trumpet at school.

gloss.en: able to be trusted or relied on

words.example: She is a kind and trustworthy friend.

gloss.en: the true facts about something

words.example: Do you think she's telling the truth?

gloss.en: a long hollow pipe for liquids or gases

words.example: He was fed through a feeding tube for months.

gloss.en: to fall suddenly and in a clumsy way

words.example: The boy tumbled down the stairs.

gloss.en: a large sea fish eaten as food

words.example: I made a tuna sandwich for lunch.

gloss.en: sudden violent movement of air or water

words.example: The plane shook because of strong turbulence.

gloss.en: a place where one road meets another

words.example: Take the first turning on the left.

gloss.en: a sea animal with a hard shell

words.example: We saw a turtle swimming near the beach.

gloss.en: one of two children born at the same birth

words.example: His twin brother looks exactly like him.

gloss.en: a group sharing the same qualities; a kind

words.example: The doctor checked her blood type.

gloss.en: having the usual features of its kind

words.example: We ate at a typical Italian cafe.

gloss.en: in a way that usually happens

words.example: These chips are typically used for databases.

gloss.en: a rubber ring around a wheel's edge

words.example: The front tyre on my bike is flat.

gloss.en: unpleasant to look at

words.example: The building had an ugly grey front.

gloss.en: not having the ability to do something

words.example: He was unable to find work after the war.

gloss.en: not feeling fear

words.example: She was unafraid to speak the truth.

gloss.en: too surprising or extreme to believe

words.example: The view from the top was unbelievable.

gloss.en: not easy to understand or be sure about

words.example: It is still unclear who will lead the team.

gloss.en: not pleasant to wear, sit on, or feel

words.example: These new shoes are really uncomfortable.

gloss.en: impossible to control or stop
gloss.zh: 无法控制的；难以控制的

words.example: She had an uncontrollable fit of laughter.

gloss.en: to find out something hidden or secret

words.example: Police uncovered a plan to rob the bank.

gloss.en: too young to do something legally

words.example: The bar does not serve underage customers.

gloss.en: underwear worn under trousers

words.example: He packed clean underpants for the trip.

gloss.en: to open or untie something that is fastened

words.example: Please undo the top button of your shirt.

gloss.en: to take your clothes off

words.example: The child began to undress for a bath.

gloss.en: without a job but able to work

words.example: How long have you been unemployed?

gloss.en: the state of people not having jobs

words.example: This is an area of high unemployment.

gloss.en: in a surprising or unplanned way

words.example: He arrived unexpectedly late at night.

gloss.en: not right or equal; not just

words.example: They had been given an unfair advantage.

gloss.en: in a way that is not fair

words.example: He felt he was treated unfairly at work.

gloss.en: not good enough or not healthy enough

words.example: He is too unfit to run a race.

gloss.en: not kind or pleasant to people

words.example: The staff were cold and unfriendly.

gloss.en: not listened to or noticed

words.example: Her warnings went unheard by the team.

gloss.en: not showing interest in something

words.example: He seemed uninterested in the lesson.

gloss.en: not interesting; dull

words.example: The film was long and uninteresting.

gloss.en: an organization protecting workers' rights

words.example: I've joined the union at my workplace.

gloss.en: except if; if not

words.example: You won't get paid unless you have a note.

gloss.en: different from a person or thing

words.example: Music is quite unlike any other art form.

gloss.en: not likely to happen; not probable

words.example: The project seemed unlikely to succeed.

gloss.en: having or bringing bad luck

words.example: He was unlucky to miss the train by a minute.

gloss.en: not needed; more than is needed

words.example: They caused unnecessary suffering to animals.

gloss.en: to take things out of a bag or box

words.example: Let me unpack my bag after the trip.

gloss.en: not pleasant or comfortable

words.example: Losing the keys was an unpleasant surprise.

gloss.en: changing so you cannot know what will happen
gloss.zh: 不可预测的；难以预料的

words.example: The weather here is very unpredictable.

gloss.en: not connected to something else

words.example: The two crimes are completely unrelated.

gloss.en: not neat; in a mess

words.example: His bedroom is always untidy.

gloss.en: up to a point in time or an event

words.example: Let's wait until the rain stops.

gloss.en: in a way that is not normal or common

words.example: It was unusually warm for winter.

gloss.en: not wanted or needed

words.example: She gave away her unwanted clothes.

gloss.en: not feeling healthy; ill

words.example: She felt unwell and stayed home.

gloss.en: modern; having the latest information

words.example: Please keep your records up-to-date.

gloss.en: feeling happier and more hopeful

words.example: The music left her feeling uplifted.

gloss.en: to send files to a computer or website

words.example: I will upload the photos to the website.

gloss.en: on or onto something

words.example: The decision rests upon you alone.

gloss.en: unhappy or worried about something bad

words.example: I understand how upset you must be feeling.

gloss.en: on a higher floor of a building

words.example: They cleaned the upstairs bathroom.

gloss.en: moving or pointing to a higher place

words.example: There was an upward movement in prices.

gloss.en: familiar with something done often

words.example: I'm not used to eating so much at lunchtime.

gloss.en: a job or room that is available

words.example: The hotel had one vacancy for the night.

gloss.en: too proud of your looks or abilities

words.example: He is too vain to wear glasses.

gloss.en: worth a lot of money

words.example: My home is my most valuable asset.

gloss.en: a covered vehicle for carrying goods

words.example: The driver of a white van parked outside.

gloss.en: several different sorts of one thing

words.example: There is a wide variety of patterns to choose from.

gloss.en: several different

words.example: The shop sells various types of bread.

gloss.en: not eating meat or fish

words.example: She ordered a vegetarian meal.

gloss.en: a machine like a car used for transport

words.example: Motor vehicles are not allowed in the park.

gloss.en: a doctor who treats animals

words.example: We took the sick cat to the vet.

gloss.en: a person hurt by a crime or accident

words.example: Police are helping the victim of the crime.

gloss.en: what you can see from a place

words.example: There were magnificent views of the countryside.

gloss.en: using physical force to hurt or kill

words.example: The city has a lot of violent crime.

gloss.en: in a way using strong physical force

words.example: The door shook violently in the storm.

gloss.en: a tiny thing that causes disease

words.example: He caught the flu virus last week.

gloss.en: in a way related to seeing

words.example: The book is visually very attractive.

gloss.en: producing clear, strong, lively pictures

words.example: She gave a vivid description of the city.

gloss.en: in a clear and lively way

words.example: I vividly remember my first day at school.

gloss.en: a mountain that can send out lava

words.example: The volcano erupted and sent ash into the sky.

gloss.en: a formal choice made in an election

words.example: Every vote counts in this election.

gloss.en: a speech sound like a, e, i, o, u

words.example: The letter 'a' is a vowel.

gloss.en: a long journey by sea or in space

words.example: They made a long voyage across the ocean.

gloss.en: a vehicle with four wheels pulled by horses

words.example: The farmer loaded hay onto the waggon.

gloss.en: a vehicle with four wheels for carrying loads

words.example: The horses pulled the heavy wagon up the hill.

gloss.en: a large cupboard for hanging clothes

words.example: She hung her coat in the wardrobe.

gloss.en: at a pleasant fairly high temperature

words.example: We enjoyed the warm weather at the beach.

gloss.en: in a friendly or warm way

words.example: She greeted her guests warmly.

gloss.en: the quality of being warm or friendly

words.example: We felt the warmth of the fire.

gloss.en: to tell someone about a possible danger

words.example: I tried to warn him, but he wouldn't listen.

gloss.en: a statement that something bad may happen

words.example: I had absolutely no warning of the storm.
